old_claim.note: Advanced Distillation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Fractional distillation is the core separation method that turns crude petroleum into boiling-range fractions such as gasoline, kerosene, diesel, and bitumen.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica describes distillation as the refining process used to separate petroleum into gasoline, kerosene, diesel fuel, bitumen, and other products.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source presents distillation as the method that separates petroleum into useful product fractions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports an enabling edge because petroleum refining's defining operation is adapted industrial distillation rather than a generic chemical context.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: distillation_advanced remains an enabling edge with textbook support.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to cracking, reforming, or hydrotreating rather than fractional distillation.
A stronger source showed early petroleum refining did not depend on distillation practice.
A more precise fractional_distillation node replaced distillation_advanced as the prerequisite.
short_reason: Distillation is the core petroleum-separation method.
